name: ibuprofen
why_interesting: In-coverage drug (SPICE); Phase 0-5 baseline. Used to verify the whole pipeline. AL does not trigger naturally even at 600 K (REPORT §5-9).

name: sulfanilamide
why_interesting: Sulfonamide + aryl amine; MACE-OFF baseline RMSE_F is 2.5x higher than ibuprofen. Cross-molecule validation (§10) and Phase-5 long-run demo (§12.5). 5-cycle AL completes cleanly with cloud + safeguard.

name: glycine_zwitterion
why_interesting: Net-neutral zwitterion (NH3+/COO-) on a tiny molecule; SPICE under-represents zwitterionic forms. First Phase-6 candidate (§12.7). GFN-FF handles natively.

name: sulfonyl_chloride
why_interesting: Methanesulfonyl chloride; hypervalent S + Cl in the same group. SPICE has little of this. Phase-6 backup candidate.

name: diglycine
why_interesting: Glycyl-glycine (peptide bond model); intermediate-flexibility peptide. Phase-6 candidate for testing AL on backbone phi/psi exploration.

name: n_methylacetamide
why_interesting: NMA — classic peptide-bond model compound used in many AMBER/CHARMM benchmarks. Probably in MACE-OFF coverage but cheap to test as a sanity check.

name: biphenyl
why_interesting: Famous inter-ring torsion barrier (~2-5 kcal/mol, minimum at ~45 deg). Classic FF benchmark. Likely in-coverage but tests aromatic-aromatic torsion handling.

name: norbornadiene
why_interesting: Strained bicyclic dihydrocarbon; unusual bond angles. Tests MACE-OFF on strained-ring chemistry.

name: caffeine
why_interesting: Multiple fused rings + methylated nitrogens. Standard drug-like benchmark; likely in coverage.
